facts.label: THE HERO OF KIGALI
facts.story: Angola went to Rwanda on 8 October 2005 needing a result to reach a first-ever World Cup, while rivals Nigeria were routing Zimbabwe 5–1 in a parallel kickoff. In the 79th minute, Zé Kalanga swung in a cross and striker Akwá rose to head it home. The goal held, Angola won the group on head-to-head record, and Akwá became forever known at home as the hero of Kigali.

facts.label: PALANCAS NEGRAS
facts.story: Angola's players carry the nickname Palancas Negras, the Black Sable Antelopes, after the giant sable antelope, a critically rare species found only in Angola and treated as a national emblem of endurance. When Puma built the squad's first-ever World Cup kit in 2006, it dressed the team in red with bold black and yellow stripes lifted straight from the national flag, a jersey still remembered as one of that tournament's most striking debuts.

2006.headline: One Goal To Remember

annotations.label: A GENTLE WELCOME TO THE STAGE
annotations.story: In Angola's first-ever World Cup match, against a Portugal side making Cristiano Ronaldo's tournament debut, Pauleta's finish was enough to settle it 1–0. Ties between the two nations ran deep, and Cologne's stadium had an unusually warm, almost celebratory atmosphere for a group-stage elimination match.

annotations.label: THE UNEMPLOYED KEEPER'S SHUTOUT
annotations.story: João Ricardo, the only player in the tournament without a club, produced a man-of-the-match display to help Angola hold Mexico scoreless in Hanover. The 0–0 draw kept alive a slim chance of advancing and stood as the defensive high point of the country's World Cup debut.

annotations.label: THE ONLY GOAL THAT COUNTS
annotations.story: With qualification already gone, Flávio rose in the 60th minute in Leipzig to head home a cross from the right, leveling Iran 1–1 and scoring the only goal Angola has ever notched at a World Cup. Sohrab Bakhtiarizadeh's earlier strike for Iran couldn't be matched, but Angola left Germany unbeaten in two of three.

2006.homeColors: red shirt with black and yellow horizontal stripes across the chest, mirroring the Angolan flag (Puma)
2006.awayColors: white shirt with red and black trim (Puma)
